commit.message:
feat: replace Docker mock sine waves with simplex noise (#46)

## Summary

- Replace sine-wave + bucket-noise mock data for Docker containers with
multi-octave 2D simplex noise (`simplex-noise` library, ~2KB gzipped)
- Each container metric uses 4 layered noise octaves (slow drift +
medium variation + fine detail + micro-jitter) for organic,
non-repeating charts
- Anti-alias at coarse zoom levels by clamping octave periods to the
Nyquist limit
- Match real DB behavior: 300 target points for container history (was
5000), suppress CPU spikes at coarse sample intervals
- Cache noise functions per seed to avoid rebuilding permutation tables
on every call

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `package.json` | Add `simplex-noise@4.0.3` |
| `src/lib/mock/patterns.ts` | Add `SimplexProfile` interface,
`generateSimplexMetric()` with caching and Nyquist clamping |
| `src/lib/mock/__tests__/patterns.test.ts` | 7 new tests for
`generateSimplexMetric` |
| `src/lib/mock/entities.ts` | `DockerEntityDef` uses `SimplexProfile`;
all 10 containers tuned |
| `src/lib/mock/generators/docker.ts` | Use `generateSimplexMetric`,
pass `sampleIntervalMs`, 300-point default |
| `src/lib/mock/functions/docker.functions.ts` | Pass `targetPoints`
through to generator |

ZFS (ActivityProfile) and Proxmox (MetricProfile) are unchanged.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run typecheck` passes
- [x] All 744 tests pass (90 mock-specific)
- [x] 7 new tests for `generateSimplexMetric` (determinism, bounds,
variation, custom weights)
- [x] Visual verification: demo build charts show smooth organic curves
at all zoom levels (1H, 1D, 7D, 30D)
- [x] Tooltip responsiveness at 30-day range (300 points vs previous
5000)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Map: classified 6b-6e as predicted fails, added 6f meta-theorem and 6g frontier

6b (2-adic): fixed-point eq IS cycle eq, tools repackage not escape
6c (polynomial): finite-field encoding loses size info (the 5% margin)
6d (Subspace Thm): effective height bounds = Baker = abc barrier
6e (thermodynamic): rigorous pressure = rigorous expected count = abc
6f: META-THEOREM — any size-counting method hits abc
6g: STRUCTURE-EXPLOITING METHODS — the true frontier
    Must use specific algebraic structure of S(I) = Σ 3^{k-j}·2^{i_j}
    (geometric coefficients, ordered subsets, elementary symmetric fn)
    not just its size. No known framework does this at required scale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: sign Go binaries before placing in app bundle

codesign resolves Contents/MacOS/agentsh to the parent bundle,
causing it to check all subcomponents. Fix by creating universal
binaries in a staging dir, signing them there, then copying the
pre-signed binaries into the app bundle during assembly.

commit.message:
fix: Media 組件 fill 模式外層容器缺少 w-full h-full 導致圖片高度為 0

當 Media 以 fill 模式使用時（如訂單詳情頁商品縮圖），外層 Tag 沒有
w-full h-full，導致內層 relative 容器的 h-full 解析為 0，NextImage
absolute 定位的圖片因此不可見。

filters, not type checker functions. Make contains_atomic return
Option<bool> for consistent tri-state semantics throughout. Extract
tri_any helper for Sum variant matching. Callers use unwrap_or(false)
so indeterminate types are excluded from autocomplete suggestions.

commit.message:
Implement SQLite-backed consensus engine with full proposal state machine

Replace in-memory ConsensusEngine with a database-backed implementation that
manages proposals and reviews through SQLite. Enhanced schema adds content,
required_reviewers, source_task_id, and max_rounds to proposals table, and
introduces a new reviews table with per-round uniqueness constraints. The
engine provides create/review/query APIs and automatic round evaluation
(all-approve→APPROVED, escalate→ESCALATED, reject with rounds left→next round,
reject on final round→ESCALATED).

commit.message:
Fix thumbnails missing after back navigation and unreliable pause button (#56)

Two bugs in the state machine's effect emissions:

1. The "back" event (detail → browsing) only emitted renderBrowsingList,
   but not fetchListSummaries. Since the detail view replaces the entire
   DOM, the list rebuilds from scratch without thumbnails/descriptions.

2. The "togglePause" event when unpausing only emitted requery. If
   queryResult found the same articles, only updateDistances ran — the
   header (with the pause button state) was never re-rendered.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: VS Code window display — single visible window per connection, disable RENDER

- Remove pending_show_frames (deferred window visibility) — show windows immediately
- Electron/VS Code creates 4 top-level windows: create native macOS windows for all
  (needed as IOSurface render targets) but only show the first one per connection
- Subsequent windows from same connection stay hidden (render-only buffers)
- WM_TRANSIENT_FOR windows (dialogs) always shown
- Disable RENDER extension advertisement — GTK uses RENDER for all drawing,
  causing blank dialog windows since RENDER ops are not implemented
- Polling click targeting: use windowNumber (macOS Z-order) instead of area heuristic
